name: Quebec-Style Roast Goose
recipeInstructions:
1. Make stuffing by combining bread, currants, apples, thyme, salt, pepper and melted butter.
2. Stuff the goose with this mixture, truss and tie securely.
3. Prick the bird all over with a fork.
4. Heat oil in a roasting pan on top of the stove.
5. Brown the goose lightly on all sides, then drain off pan drippings.
6. Set the goose breast side up, add a little water, and cover.
7. Roast at 375 degrees for one hour.
8. Combine chopped onion, carrot, celery, garlic, bay leaf, cloves, thyme, and marjoram in a separate bowl.
9. Discard any fat from the roasting pan.
10. Add the vegetable mixture and continue roasting uncovered for 20-25 minutes per pound (total of three to four hours).
11. Transfer the cooked goose to a platter and keep warm.
12. Skim off remaining fat in the pan and heat dripping and vegetables on top of the stove until the mixture is reduced.
13. Stir in white wine, tomato paste, and chicken bouillon. Simmer for 10-15 minutes.
14. Strain the gravy.
15. If desired, thicken the gravy by blending a little cornstarch with water.
16. Serve the goose with gravy, applesauce, mashed potatoes, and braised cabbage. Makes six to eight servings.
